mission.title: Install and Configure CloudNativePG (PostgreSQL Operator)
mission.description: CloudNativePG is an operator for managing PostgreSQL databases within Kubernetes, automating the operational lifecycle from deployment to maintenance. It is ideal for teams looking to integrate PostgreSQL management into their Kubernetes workflows with a focus on GitOps and automation. Key trade-offs include the need for Kubernetes expertise and the reliance on Kubernetes-native features for high availability.

steps.title: Add the CloudNativePG Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the official CloudNativePG Helm chart repository to your Helm configuration.

```bash

helm repo add cloudnative-pg https://charts.cloudnative-pg.io

```

> This URL points to the official Helm chart repository.

steps.title: Install the Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs)
steps.description:
CRDs are necessary for the operator to manage PostgreSQL clusters effectively.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/cloudnative-pg/cloudnative-pg/v1.28.1/deploy/crds.yaml

```

> This command installs the required CRDs for CloudNativePG.

steps.title: Install the CloudNativePG operator
steps.description:
This step installs the CloudNativePG operator in the specified namespace.

```bash

helm install cloudnative-pg cloudnative-pg/cloudnative-pg --namespace cloudnative-pg --create-namespace --version 1.28.1

```

> This installs the operator in a new namespace called 'cloudnative-pg'.

steps.title: Create a PostgreSQL cluster
steps.description:
This step creates a PostgreSQL cluster using a custom resource definition.

```bash

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: postgres.cloudnative-pg.io/v1
kind: Cluster
metadata:
  name: my-postgres
spec:
  instances: 3
  postgresql:
    version: '15'
EOF

```

> This creates a 3-instance PostgreSQL cluster named 'my-postgres'.
commands:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: postgres.cloudnative-pg.io/v1
kind: Cluster
metadata:
  name: my-postgres
spec:
  instances: 3
  postgresql:
    version: '15'
EOF

steps.title: Verify the PostgreSQL cluster status
steps.description:
This step checks the status of the PostgreSQL cluster to ensure it is running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get clusters -n cloudnative-pg

```

> You should see 'my-postgres' with a status of 'Running'.

uninstall.title: Uninstall the CloudNativePG operator
uninstall.description:
This step removes the CloudNativePG operator and its associated resources. Be aware that this will delete the PostgreSQL clusters and any associated data.

```bash

helm uninstall cloudnative-pg --namespace cloudnative-pg

kubectl delete namespace cloudnative-pg

```

> This command uninstalls the operator.
> This command deletes the namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade the CloudNativePG operator
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you have a backup of your PostgreSQL data. This step will upgrade the operator to the latest version.

```bash

kubectl get clusters -n cloudnative-pg -o json | jq '.items[] | .metadata.name' | xargs -I {} kubectl drain {} --ignore-daemonsets --delete-local-data

helm upgrade cloudnative-pg cloudnative-pg/cloudnative-pg --namespace cloudnative-pg --version 1.28.1

kubectl get clusters -n cloudnative-pg

```

> Drain the PostgreSQL pods before upgrading.
> This command upgrades the operator to version 1.28.1.
> Verify that the clusters are running correctly after the upgrade.
commands: kubectl get clusters -n cloudnative-pg -o json | jq '.items[] | .metadata.name' | xargs -I {} kubectl drain {} --ignore-daemonsets --delete-local-data

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to create cluster: CRD not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs) may not be installed.

**Fix:**
Run 'kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/cloudnative-pg/cloudnative-pg/v1.28.1/deploy/crds.yaml' to install the CRDs.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'PostgreSQL cluster is not ready'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The PostgreSQL pods may be in a crash loop or not starting properly.

**Fix:**
Check the logs of the pods using 'kubectl logs <pod-name> -n cloudnative-pg' to diagnose the issue.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient resources to schedule pod'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The cluster may not have enough resources available.

**Fix:**
Ensure that your Kubernetes nodes have sufficient resources (minimum 2 vCPU, 4GB RAM per node).

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to connect to PostgreSQL'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The PostgreSQL service may not be accessible due to network policies or service misconfiguration.

**Fix:**
Check the service configuration with 'kubectl get svc -n cloudnative-pg' and ensure network policies allow access.

versionNotes.changes: Improved support for PostgreSQL 15, added new monitoring features.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see the PostgreSQL cluster 'my-postgres' listed with a status of 'Running' when you run 'kubectl get clusters -n cloudnative-pg'.

EOF

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with the specified resources and the necessary tools installed.
